parties.display_name: Potomac Capital Partners II, L. P.

cohorts.label: 18 prospective acquirers contacted by BofA Merrill Lynch
three prospective acquirers that submitted written indications of interest for a complete purchase of sTec
nine prospective acquirers not interested in a potential transaction

observations.summary: Management and sTec board regularly reviewed and assessed strategy with goal of maximizing shareholder value, considering strategic alternatives including a sale of the company.
observations.other_detail: board review of strategic alternatives over the last few years

observations.summary: Board determined to undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives, including exploring a potential sale of sTec. In mid-November 2012, board authorized management to contact potential financial advisors.
An investment bank representing Company A contacted sTec management to request a preliminary meeting. Meeting was subsequently cancelled due to concerns by non-management board members.
Balch Hill Partners reported acquisition of 6.4% of sTec's outstanding shares and filed Schedule 13D with the SEC. Later joined by Potomac Capital Partners, jointly increasing holdings to 9.8%.
observations.other_detail: activist stake disclosure

observations.summary: Balch Hill filed amendment to Schedule 13D and issued letter to sTec board urging cost reductions, SAS interface focus, and exploration of strategic alternatives.
observations.other_detail: activist letter urging strategic alternatives

observations.summary: Board established special committee (Dr. Daly, Messrs. Colpitts and Witte) to conduct review of strategic alternatives including possible sale. Expanded to four members on March 26, 2013.
observations.other_detail: special committee formation

observations.summary: Company B President met with Mr. Manouch Moshayedi and stated intention to present sTec as a possible acquisition target to Company B's management.
Approximately two weeks after February 13, Company B's management determined it was not interested in acquiring sTec and believed it could independently develop the needed technology.
Representatives of Company C expressed a potential interest in exploring an acquisition of particular assets of sTec's business at a meeting with Messrs. Moshayedi.
In mid-March 2013, the head of corporate development for Company D contacted sTec management to express interest in exploring a potential acquisition of the company.

Board approved retaining BofA Merrill Lynch as sTec's financial advisor for general review of strategic alternatives and defensive preparedness. Board determined to confidentially approach strategic buyers.
observations.other_detail: board approved retaining financial advisor

observations.summary: sTec entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch to provide financial advisory services.
observations.other_detail: engagement letter for financial advisory services

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting potential acquirers. In total, discussed with 18 prospective acquirers (17 tech companies and 1 financial sponsor). Three submitted written IOIs for complete purchase, six interested only in select assets, nine not interested.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G.
sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.
observations.other_detail: addendum to existing non-disclosure agreement

observations.summary: Company C indicated it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of the company. Special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company C.
Company F declined the invitation to schedule a management presentation, indicating it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec. No further communications followed.
Shortly after April 24, Company E indicated it was also only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec. Special committee decided not to continue discussions.
BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.
Company D provided a verbal indication of its interest to pursue a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share.
observations.other_detail: verbal indication of interest; Company D detailed additional diligence requirements for submitting a written indication

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch sent a process letter to Company G requesting a non-binding indication of interest by May 3, 2013.
Company H contacted BofA Merrill Lynch expressing an interest in a potential transaction with sTec.
WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire sTec at a purchase price in the range of $6.60-$7.10 per share in cash.
Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.
Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire sTec at $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.
observations.other_detail: submitted along with a draft exclusivity agreement

observations.summary: Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire sTec at a purchase price in the range of $5.00-$5.75 per share in cash.
BofA Merrill Lynch informed Company D it would be allowed to continue in the process with the understanding that Company D would need to meaningfully increase its proposal.
observations.other_detail: informed Company D must meaningfully increase its proposal

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch contacted Company H and indicated that the price range Company H had submitted was not sufficient to move them forward in the process. Company H was told it could submit a revised indication of interest.
observations.other_detail: Company H price range deemed insufficient

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.
Company H contacted BofA Merrill Lynch indicating it remained interested in a potential acquisition but was not able to increase its indicated value range.
observations.other_detail: cannot improve price

observations.summary: WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at $9.15 per share in cash, with a mark-up of the merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements.
observations.other_detail: second-round indication of interest with mark-up of merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements; contemplated signing by June 3, 2013

observations.summary: Company D indicated it continued to have interest in a potential acquisition but needed approximately two weeks of additional time to continue due diligence and submit a written indication of interest and mark-up of merger agreement.
observations.other_detail: requested additional time for due diligence

observations.summary: Board directed BofA Merrill Lynch to request a best and final proposal from WDC and a best and final written proposal from Company D by May 30, 2013.
WDC verbally indicated that its $9.15 per share offer was its best possible price, and reiterated WDC's concern regarding the price.
observations.other_detail: WDC verbally confirmed $9.15 as best possible price; reiterated concern regarding the price

observations.summary: Company D reconfirmed it would require additional time to submit a written indication of interest but indicated it continued to believe it could be competitive on price if given additional time.
observations.other_detail: requested additional time; indicated could be competitive on price

observations.summary: Board unanimously determined to move forward with WDC on their proposed terms, including endeavoring to announce a transaction on June 3, 2013. BofA Merrill Lynch informed WDC of board's decision to proceed at $9.15 per share.
observations.other_detail: board decision to move forward with WDC

observations.summary: WDC communicated it was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec and was not prepared to move forward with a transaction at that time. WDC discontinued due diligence efforts.
observations.other_detail: WDC reevaluating interest; not prepared to move forward

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch informed Company D that timing for sTec's process had been delayed and that Company D had an opportunity to continue in the process.
observations.other_detail: Company D informed of opportunity to continue in process

observations.summary: Company D contacted BofA Merrill Lynch and indicated it would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process.

WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest at $6.60-$7.10 per share in cash, down from $9.15, citing internal disagreement, non-essential view of the acquisition, and business factors affecting sTec.
observations.other_detail: revised indication of interest; WDC communicated it had given serious consideration to not proceeding and lowered price due to business factors and change in bidding strategy

observations.summary: Board directed BofA Merrill Lynch to communicate disappointment in WDC's latest proposal and demand WDC submit a specific proposal reflecting its best offer with senior management confirmation.
WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash, described as WDC's best and final price, and indicated it could announce a transaction on June 24, 2013.
observations.other_detail: WDC stated $6.85 was best and final price; otherwise on terms previously proposed May 28

observations.summary: Special committee unanimously determined to move forward with WDC at $6.85 per share and recommended the same to the board. Board unanimously determined to move forward with WDC on those terms.
observations.other_detail: special committee and board decision to accept WDC terms

observations.summary: sTec and WDC entered into a clean team agreement to allow certain representatives of WDC access to further information in the data room.

sTec, WDC, and Merger Sub executed the merger agreement. Officers and directors executed voting agreements. Moshayedis executed covenant not to sue, consulting agreements, non-competition agreements, and related ancillary agreements.
Board unanimously approved and adopted the merger agreement. BofA Merrill Lynch delivered fairness opinion that the $6.85 per share merger consideration was fair from a financial point of view.
WDC and sTec announced in a joint press release the execution of the merger agreement, prior to the opening of trading on U.S. stock exchanges.
observations.other_detail: public announcement of merger

observations.summary: Balch Hill filed an amendment to its Schedule 13D and issued a letter urging sTec board to reduce costs and explore strategic alternatives
sTec board determined to confidentially approach strategic buyers most likely to have interest in a potential acquisition, and to conduct a limited confirmatory exploration of financial buyers
WDC communicated it was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec and was not prepared to move forward with a transaction at that time
Board held special meeting where Dr. Daly reported WDC had decided to reconsider its interest in acquiring sTec and was not prepared to move forward

exclusions.explanation: BofA Merrill Lynch financial analyses detail (selected publicly traded companies, precedent transactions, DCF, premiums paid): Valuation methodology detail from fairness opinion section (w2/w3) does not constitute process observations; these are advisor analyses, not chronological deal events.

BofA Merrill Lynch fee arrangement ($4.3 million) and conflict of interest disclosures: Advisor fee disclosures and prior relationship disclosures (w2/w3) are not process observations; they are standard proxy disclosure requirements.

Board reasons for the merger (attractive value, high likelihood of completion, receipt of fairness opinion, etc.): Board rationale factors (w1/w2) describe the board's reasoning, not chronological events. The actual decision is captured in obs_evt_049.
Severance and change in control agreements (March 11, 2013 with Cook and Saman): Employment/compensation arrangements are not M&A process observations under the v2 schema.

Standstill/dont-ask-dont-waive provision discussions: NDA provision details captured contextually in quotes Q063-Q065 but the discussions about waiver are negotiation detail, not separate observations. The NDA agreements themselves are captured.
